events.title: Russo-Japanese War
events.description: Conflict over influence in Manchuria and Korea ends in Japanese victory, signaling Japan's emergence as a major power.

events.title: Xinhai Revolution
events.description: Revolution ends Qing dynasty and leads to the establishment of the Republic of China, reshaping Chinese politics and society.

events.title: World War I
events.description: Global conflict centered in Europe involving major powers and industrialized warfare, resulting in massive casualties and political upheaval.

events.title: Russian Revolution and Civil War
events.description: Bolshevik Revolution leads to the collapse of the Russian Empire and the establishment of the Soviet state after civil war.

events.title: Spanish flu pandemic
events.description: Influenza pandemic causes tens of millions of deaths worldwide in the aftermath of World War I, with major social and public health impacts.

events.title: Great Depression begins
events.description: Severe global economic downturn triggered by the US stock market crash leads to mass unemployment, political instability, and policy innovations.

events.title: World War II
events.description: Largest global conflict in history with theaters across Europe, Asia, Africa, and the Pacific; results in massive destruction, Holocaust, and reshaping of world order.

events.title: India and Pakistan gain independence
events.description: British India is partitioned into India and Pakistan; independence achieved amid mass migration and communal violence.

events.title: People's Republic of China proclaimed
events.description: Chinese Communist Party proclaims the PRC after victory in the civil war, establishing communist rule over mainland China.

events.title: Korean War
events.description: Conflict on the Korean Peninsula between North and South Korea with international involvement, resulting in armistice and continued division.

events.title: Sputnik launched
events.description: Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite, initiating the space age and intensifying the Cold War space race.

events.title: Civil Rights Act (USA)
events.description: Major US legislation outlawing discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin, a landmark in American civil rights history.

events.title: Apollo 11 Moon landing
events.description: NASA's Apollo 11 mission lands humans on the Moon for the first time, marking a major milestone in space exploration.

events.title: Bangladesh independence
events.description: East Pakistan becomes independent Bangladesh after a war with Pakistan, reshaping South Asian geopolitics.

events.title: Iranian Revolution
events.description: Revolution overthrows the Shah and establishes the Islamic Republic under Ayatollah Khomeini, transforming Middle Eastern politics.

events.title: Fall of the Berlin Wall
events.description: Opening and later dismantling of the Berlin Wall symbolizes the end of Cold War divisions and leads to German reunification and broader geopolitical change.

events.title: Tiananmen Square protests
events.description: Large pro-democracy demonstrations in Beijing are suppressed by the Chinese government, with significant political and human rights consequences.

events.title: 1905 Russian Revolution and global ripple effects
events.description: Mass unrest, strikes, and political agitation force limited reforms in the Russian Empire and inspire movements across Eurasia.

events.title: Xinhai Revolution ends Qing dynasty
events.description: Revolutionary uprisings topple the Qing dynasty and lead to the Republic of China, reshaping Chinese political life.

events.title: Jallianwala Bagh massacre and Indian nationalism
events.description: British troops fire on unarmed civilians in Amritsar, intensifying Indian demands for self-rule and galvanizing the independence movement.

events.title: Warlord era and Nationalist-Communist struggle in China
events.description: Fragmentation after the Qing collapse leads to regional warlords, the rise of the Kuomintang, and later conflict with the Chinese Communist Party.

events.title: Indian independence movements intensify (Gandhi era)
events.description: Nonviolent campaigns, civil disobedience, and mass mobilization under leaders like Gandhi accelerate demands for independence from British rule.

events.title: Second Sino-Japanese War
events.description: Large-scale conflict between China and Japan causes massive civilian suffering and becomes a major theater of World War II in Asia.

events.title: Partition of British India and independence
events.description: British India is partitioned into India and Pakistan; independence accompanied by mass migration and communal violence.

events.title: People's Republic of China established
events.description: Chinese Communist Party proclaims the PRC after civil war, initiating socialist transformation and state-led development.

events.title: Decolonization movements across Africa
events.description: Nationalist movements lead to independence for many African states, reshaping global politics and ending formal colonial empires.

events.title: Sino-Indian War
events.description: Border conflict between China and India over Himalayan territories results in Chinese military gains and long-term strategic tensions.

events.title: Bangladesh independence war
events.description: East Pakistan fights a war of independence against West Pakistan, resulting in the creation of Bangladesh and major humanitarian crises.

events.title: China's Reform and Opening under Deng Xiaoping
events.description: Market-oriented reforms, special economic zones, and global integration transform China's economy and society.

events.title: Collapse of the Soviet Union and geopolitical shifts
events.description: Dissolution of the USSR reshapes Eurasian geopolitics, accelerates transitions in former Soviet republics, and affects global alignments.
